qa_pairs.question: How do you prepare and run an Ansys Workbench replay file in different directories on an Alliance cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: To use a single Ansys Workbench replay file in different directories, you must first open the replay file in a text editor and modify its internal FilePath setting. Before executing the replay file, ensure the appropriate Ansys module (e.g., `ansys/2019R3`) is loaded, remove any existing lock files by running `rm -f test_files/.lock`, and then launch Workbench with the replay file using the command `runwb2 -R myreplay.wbjn`.

qa_pairs.question: How can I check the resource utilization of an Ansys Additive Manufacturing job running on an Alliance compute node?
qa_pairs.answer: After an Ansys Additive Manufacturing job has been running for a few minutes, you can obtain a snapshot of its resource utilization on the compute node(s) by using the `srun` command. Execute the following command, replacing `myjobid` with your actual job ID: `srun --overlap --jobid=myjobid top -bn1 -u $USER | grep R | grep -v top`. This will display information such as CPU and memory usage for the running Ansys processes.
